message:
🌱 Add podman-container-tools: Podman relies in config on specific qemu version to start a mission (#1175)

@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "podman-container-tools-18111-podman-relies-in-config-on-specific-qemu-version-to",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "podman-container-tools: Podman relies in config on specific qemu version to start a machine",
+    "description": "Podman relies in config on specific qemu version to start a machine. This issue affects 11+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify podman-container-tools troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your podman-container-tools installation:\n```bash\npodman --version\npodman info --format json | jq .version\n```\nLook for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review podman-container-tools configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant podman-container-tools configuration:\n### Issue Description\n\nThe config that is set up has this line:\n\n    \"file=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/qemu/7.2.0/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd,if=pflash,format=raw,readonly=on\",\n\nHowever, my quemu has been updated to 7.2.1 and is therefore not available"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Podman relies in config on specific qemu version to start a…",
+        "description": "[This](This) reverts commit 6b6458916eaa51f0dbbcfeccd740706697697ad3 (Resolve\nsymlink path for qemu directory if possible).\n\nFully resolving the symlink to qemu solves some issues for\naarch64-darwin nix with regards to finding `edk2-aarch64-code.fd`, but\nunfortunately the fully resolved path\n```yaml\n~ podman machine start\nStarting machine \"podman-machine-default\"\nWaiting for VM ...\nError: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code 1, stderr: qemu-system-aarch64: -drive file=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/qemu/7.2.0/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd,if=pflash,format=raw,readonly=on: Could not open '/opt/homebrew/Cellar/qemu/7.2.0/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd': No such file or directory\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade podman-container-tools to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade podman-container-tools:\n```bash\n# Check current version\npodman --version\n# Follow the project's upgrade guide at https://github.com/containers/podman\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Podman relies in config on specific qemu version… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\nTest podman-container-tools to confirm the issue is resolved.\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "[This](This) reverts commit 6b6458916eaa51f0dbbcfeccd740706697697ad3 (Resolve\nsymlink path for qemu directory if possible).",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "~ podman machine start\nStarting machine \"podman-machine-default\"\nWaiting for VM ...\nError: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code 1, stderr: qemu-system-aarch64: -drive file=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/qemu/7.2.0/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd,if=pflash,format=raw,readonly=on: Could not open '/opt/homebrew/Cellar/qemu/7.2.0/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd': No such file or directory",
+        "M1 Macbook Pro has the same issue. \n\nedit:",
+        "Current `main` I get:"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
